query: Tesla one-time Full Self-Driving purchase discontinued after February 14 2026 subscription only official support page

{"query":"Tesla one-time Full Self-Driving purchase discontinued after February 14 2026 subscription only official support page","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1187,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://electrek.co/2026/01/14/tesla-tsla-stop-selling-full-self-driving-package-subscription-only/","title":"Tesla (TSLA) to stop selling Full Self-Driving package, moves to subscription-only: why it's a big move | Electrek","content":"CEO Elon Musk announced today that the automaker will stop selling FSD as a one-time option and will instead only offer it as a monthly subscription.
Tesla will stop selling FSD after Feb 14.
This move comes after a roller-coaster few years for FSD pricing that directly contradicted Musk’s claims."},{"url":"https://www.teslaoracle.com/2026/05/07/tesla-europe-issues-deadlines-for-one-time-fsd-purchase-in-favor-of-monthly-subscriptions/","title":"Tesla Europe issues deadlines for one-time FSD purchase in favor of monthly subscriptions - Tesla Oracle","content":"Additionally, the more affordable Enhanced Autopilot with limited features is also going to be discontinued this month.
The one-time FSD Capability AI self-driving software package costs €7,500 in Europe.
European users can still buy FSD for a one-time payment of €7,500 before May 15 in the Netherlands and May 21 in the rest of Europe.
If a Tesla owner subscribes to FSD for €7,500 in Europe or $7,500 in North America, the €199 or $199 per month subscription will break even in 37.6 months or around 3.1 years.
Tesla (TSLA) has added Miami, Florida, as one more..."},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/tesla-fsd-purchase-ends-tomorrow-final-24-hours-to-buy-at-8-000","title":"Tesla FSD Purchase Ends Tomorrow: Final 24 Hours to Buy at $8,000","content":"After February 14, FSD will only be available through monthly subscription, and current referral benefits offering $500 off FSD purchases will expire.
Decide by midnight Feb 14 — This is your final opportunity to own FSD outright.
For Tesla owners, the decision calculus is straightforward: the $8,000 purchase option ($7,500 with new vehicle referral) breaks even against a $99/month subscription after approximately 6.75 years of ownership.
The BASENOR Editorial Desk covers Tesla, SpaceX, and related technology, curating reporting from primary sources — official accounts, regulatory filings, and software release data."},{"url":"https://www.lowermysubs.com/blog/tesla-fsd-worth-it-2026","title":"$99/Month Tesla FSD: Worth It in 2026 or Skip It? | LowerMySubs Blog","content":"The one-time purchase option died on February 14, 2026, so the old buy-versus-subscribe debate is settled: subscription is the only door in.
At $99 per month ($49 if you already own Enhanced Autopilot), a full year runs $1,188.
FSD (Supervised) costs $99 per month as of July 2026, verified on Tesla's official support site.
The pricing picture in July 2026 looks like this:
Tesla's support page lists FSD (Supervised) as "a monthly subscription of $99," available from the Tesla app or the car's touchscreen.
New cars now ship with Traffic-Aware Cruise Control only, which Electrek and Carscoops both reported as a deliberate nudge toward the $99 subscription.
Basic Autopilot historically bundled Traffic-Aware Cruise Control and Autosteer for free, but new Model 3 and Model Y orders in North America now include cruise control only.
AI4 (HW4) cars run full FSD v14.
HW3 vehicles were frozen on FSD v12.6 from early 2025 until late June 2026, when Electrek reported Tesla began rolling out FSD v14 "Lite" (build 2026.20.5.1) to early-access HW3 cars.
Tesla ended one-time FSD purchases on February 14, 2026, at a final price of $8,000 ($2,000 as an upgrade for Enhanced Autopilot owners)."}]}

Best money market accounts. 4. Best free checking accounts. # Tesla to End One-Time FSD Purchase: Why the Shift Matters. Electric vehicle (EV) giant **Tesla** TSLA is making a major change to how it sells Full Self-Driving (FSD). The company will stop offering FSD as a one-time purchase after Feb. 14, 2026. The move reflects Tesla's evolving strategy around autonomy, revenue stability and long-standing promises for FSD. Around the same time, Tesla also cut the FSD subscription price from $199 per month to $99, per Electrek. The shift to subscriptions also fits better with Tesla's long-term financial goals. One of the most important targets is reaching 10 million active FSD subscriptions over the next decade, not purchases. By removing the one-time option, Tesla is pushing all new FSD users toward subscriptions — a metric that matters for Musk's compensation. RIVN is pricing Autonomy+ at $49.99 per month, or $2,500 as a one-time purchase, undercutting Tesla's pricing.","score":0.8377398,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.evdances.com/blogs/news/tesla-to-end-one-time-purchase-option-for-full-self-driving-software","title":"Tesla to End One-Time Purchase Option for Full Self-Driving Software – EVDANCE","content":"* 0 commentson Tesla to End One-Time Purchase Option for Full Self-Driving Software. # Tesla to End One-Time Purchase Option for Full Self-Driving Software. Tesla has confirmed that it will soon discontinue the one-time purchase option for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software, **fully shifting to a subscription-only model starting next month**. The change, announced by CEO Elon Musk, marks a meaningful shift in how Tesla monetizes its advanced driver-assistance technology. Tesla has offered FSD as a monthly subscription since late 2022, but buyers were still able to purchase a lifetime unlock alongside it. **That option will soon be removed entirely.** While Tesla has not officially explained the decision, the move closely mirrors a broader industry shift toward recurring software revenue. At its current pricing, **FSD has been a difficult sell as a one-time purchase**. **Some buyers were drawn in by Tesla’s robotaxi vision but delayed paying for FSD due to its high cost.** With Tesla moving to a subscription-only model, the long-term value proposition for those HW3 owners is now increasingly uncertain.","score":0.8358192,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.basenor.com/blogs/news/tesla-fsd-one-time-purchase-is-gone-what-owners-need-to-know","title":"Tesla FSD One-Time Purchase Is Gone: What Owners Need to Know","content":"# Tesla FSD One-Time Purchase Is Gone: What Owners Need to Know. Tesla has now removed the one-time FSD purchase option in the **Netherlands**, where the €7,500 outright purchase has been replaced exclusively by a **€99/month subscription** (~$115). According to The Tesla Newswire, the rollout won't stop there — **the rest of Europe is set to follow on May 21, 2026**. European Tesla owners still considering a one-time purchase have a narrow window before the subscription-only model becomes universal across the continent. If you've been sitting on the fence about buying Full Self-Driving outright, that window has closed — at least in North America, and now across most of Europe too. Here's the full picture and what it means for your Tesla. In the UK and most other European markets, the window closes **May 21, 2026**, priced at £3,400 for those who still want to act before the cutoff. For owners in the UK and most of Europe, the one-time purchase option remains available until **May 21, 2026**.","score":0.81204927,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.teslaacessories.com/blogs/news/tesla-ends-one-time-fsd-purchase-%E2%80%94-why-the-subscription-shift-matters-for-owners","title":"Tesla Ends One-Time FSD Purchase — Why the Subscription Shift Matters for Owners\n  \n  – TESMAG","content":"## Introduction: A Turning Point for Tesla Software Strategy In January 2026, Tesla confirmed a major change to one of its most controversial and closely watched products: Full Self-Driving (FSD). Beginning February 14, 2026, Tesla will no longer offer FSD as a one-time purchase for new buyers. Instead, FSD will be available exclusively through a monthly subscription, priced at approximately $99 per month in the United States, with regional pricing variations expected across Europe. ## This decision marks a fundamental shift in how Tesla monetizes software, how owners access advanced driver-assistance features, and how the company positions itself in the increasingly competitive global EV market. For Tesla owners in the U.S. and Europe, this is not just a pricing update—it affects vehicle ownership economics, resale value, long-term usability, and trust in Tesla’s autonomy roadmap. ### 1.1 The End of the One-Time FSD Purchase.
